ddls:
CREATE TABLE "system_metrics" (
  "sysID" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "sysTime" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the metric collection */,
  "sysCPUUsage" REAL /* CPU usage percentage at the time of collection */,
  "sysUptime" INTEGER /* Uptime of the system in seconds */,
  "sysPhysicalMemory" REAL /* Total physical memory in GB */,
  "sysFreeMemory" REAL /* Free memory available in GB */,
  "sysTotalThreads" INTEGER /* Total number of threads running */,
  "sysTotalProcesses" INTEGER /* Total number of processes running */,
  "sysDiskUsage" REAL /* Disk usage percentage at the time of collection */,
  "sysNetworkUsage" REAL /* Network usage percentage at the time of collection */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server related to the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sysID"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_metrics_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name or identifier of the server */,
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data center housing the server */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the server */,
  "os" TEXT /* Operating system running on the server */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment of the server (e.g., prod, dev, test) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_servers_data_center_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_center_id") REFERENCES data_centers ("data_center_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for logging into the system */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, operator) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server related to the alert */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert */,
  "alert_timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., critical, warning) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Whether the alert has been resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the platform */,
  "access_time" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., login, logout) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_centers" (
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the data center */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the data center */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries related to the data center */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number for inquiries related to the data center */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_center_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the threshold */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric to which the threshold applies (e.g., CPU Usage) */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Value at which the alert should trigger */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Alert level (e.g., warning, critical) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the threshold */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "historical_data" (
  "historical_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical data entry */,
  "sysID" INTEGER /* Reference to the metric entry from system_metrics */,
  "sysTime" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the historical metric collection */,
  "sysCPUUsage" REAL /* CPU usage percentage at the time of historical collection */,
  "sysUptime" INTEGER /* Uptime of the system in seconds */,
  "sysPhysicalMemory" REAL /* Total physical memory in GB */,
  "sysFreeMemory" REAL /* Free memory available in GB */,
  "sysTotalThreads" INTEGER /* Total number of threads running */,
  "sysTotalProcesses" INTEGER /* Total number of processes running */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("historical_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_historical_data_sysID FOREIGN KEY ("sysID") REFERENCES system_metrics ("sysID")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_windows" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance window */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server being maintained */,
  "start_time" INTEGER /* Start time of the maintenance window */,
  "end_time" INTEGER /* End time of the maintenance window */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance activity */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance activity (e.g., scheduled, in_progress, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_windows_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notification_preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification preference */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Alert level (e.g., warning, critical) */,
  "notification_method" TEXT /* Method of notification (e.g., email, sms) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notification_preferences_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_notes" (
  "note_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each note */,
  "sysID" INTEGER /* Reference to the metric entry from system_metrics */,
  "note" TEXT /* Note related to system performance */,
  "created_time" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the note was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("note_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_notes_sysID FOREIGN KEY ("sysID") REFERENCES system_metrics ("sysID")
)
